title: London is cloaking environmental racism in respectability – but Zambian villagers are fighting back
impact_evidence: Environmental | Water Resources | Surface water pollution and quality degradation -> "Hundreds of people living near Chingola were hospitalised due to water contamination from sulphuric acid and other chemicals from a KCM-owned mine in 2006." | ""We have no water source apart from the river and it's totally polluted," villager Leo Chikopela told campaign group Foil Vedanta." || Environmental | Water Resources | Freshwater ecotoxicity (heavy metals, pH changes, sensitive species) -> "Hundreds of people living near Chingola were hospitalised due to water contamination from sulphuric acid and other chemicals from a KCM-owned mine in 2006." || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Community exposure to dust, noise, pollution -> ""We have no water source apart from the river and it's totally polluted," villager Leo Chikopela told campaign group Foil Vedanta." | "Hundreds of people living near Chingola were hospitalised due to water contamination from sulphuric acid and other chemicals from a KCM-owned mine in 2006." || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Mental and physical health impacts on communities -> "Most of us are very weak and have constant stomach pains. When we bathe using this water our skin itches." || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Accidents and fatalities (including structural failures such as tailings dams) -> "Local people have also reported deaths after their relatives drank from a local river." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Anti-corruption and bribery risks -> "Vedanta has been accused of tax dodging in the past through under-pricing copper sold through KCM in order to pay less tax in Zambia." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> "The villagers were previously awarded compensation in the Zambian High Court, but KCM appealed. In 2015 the Supreme Court upheld the guilty verdict but removed the compensation." || Governance | Reputation & Social License | Social license to operate -> "Being listed on the London Stock Exchange offers companies an explicit air of credibility, because the LSX presents itself as providing strict oversight around who gets a listing and who doesn't," explains Liam Barrington-Bush." | ""In practice there is not a lot of scrutiny that these companies have to undergo, in terms of anything ethical, environmental or social, to get themselves listed.""
